old_claim.note: Scientific societies helped coordinate, promote, and publish early telescope-based astronomical work rather than merely providing a generic cultural backdrop.

new_claim_absent: No direct scientific-societies edge remains because the node is no longer scoped to Galileo/Lincei telescope publication; it is scoped to Tycho's royal-sponsored Uraniborg observatory instrument program.

source_shape.source_locator: Description and history sections: the portal describes Uraniborg under Danish royal sponsorship, purpose-built observatory structures, instruments, paper/printing facilities, and improved observing methods, without making scientific societies the institutional prerequisite.
source_shape.source_claim_summary: The corrected observatory-program scope is a royal-sponsored purpose-built observatory and instrument program, not a scientific-society publication network.
source_shape.source_support_rationale: Scientific societies may support later dissemination of telescope astronomy, but the Uraniborg program's source-backed institutional context is royal patronage, observatory infrastructure, instruments, and measurement practice.

ontology_before: Observatory Instrument Programs treated scientific societies as a direct prerequisite through a Galileo/Lincei publication framing.
ontology_after: Uraniborg Observatory Instrument Programs no longer depends on scientific societies; the scientific-society publication relationship is left to later telescope and academy nodes.
invariant_preserved_or_changed: Changed: observatory_instrument_programs no longer depends directly on scientific_societies.
would_reject_if: The node were broadened back to cover Lincei/Galileo telescope publication as the same technology.
A source showed Tycho's Uraniborg measurement program depended on a formal scientific society.
A new scientific-society observatory-publication node were added and should keep this edge.
short_reason: The corrected Uraniborg scope is not a scientific-society publication network.
